title: CrowdStrike (CRWD) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: We ran a full discounted cash flow on CrowdStrike. One number. Every assumption disclosed. Here's what CRWD is really worth. Economic Prospect Score: 87/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-lumgyrei>
CrowdStrike's revenue growth has historically exceeded 30%, but at a $3B+ run-rate, the law of large numbers applies. A 22% FCF CAGR over 5 years is highly ambitious but achievable for an apex predator in cybersecurity, as gross margins are high and scaling software requires minimal incremental capital.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-lumgyrei>
10Y Treasury: 4.18%. Using CAPM with a tech beta, the cost of equity sits around 9-11%. We use 10%. We demand a 10% annual return to justify owning a stock trading at rich multiples, protecting against multiple contraction.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale:
" data-astro-cid-lumgyrei>
Cybersecurity budgets are non-discretionary and likely to grow structurally faster than global GDP (typically 2-3%). A 4% terminal rate implies CrowdStrike remains deeply entrenched in global enterprise infrastructure essentially forever.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why is CrowdStrike's FCF so much higher than Net Income?
faq.answer: Westmount Research. "CrowdStrike (CRWD) Intrinsic Value: A DCF Analysis." westmountfundamentals.com, 2024-03-24.

prospectVerdictDetail: CrowdStrike remains a dominant force in endpoint security and threat intelligence. Despite the fallout from the July 2024 faulty software update, its underlying architecture and platform approach have proven resilient. With the recent announcement of its AWS and NVIDIA startup accelerator and high-profile industry integrations, the company continues to aggressively expand its technological footprint and market share.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: CrowdStrike maintains rapid growth, leveraging its unified cloud-native platform to displace legacy antivirus providers and consolidate multiple security functions into a single agent.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: CrowdStrike's top-line expansion continues to outpace the broader cybersecurity market, demonstrating significant momentum in cloud security, identity protection, and log management.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: It commands a leading position in endpoint detection and response (EDR). The Falcon platform is the gold standard for many large enterprises, allowing the company to continually grab share from fragmented, legacy competitors.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: While it possesses strong pricing power due to the mission-critical nature of its software, the pricing environment is increasingly competitive against bundled offerings from massive incumbents like Microsoft.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: The company releases continuous platform updates and module expansions rapidly, integrating generative AI (Charlotte AI) to automate threat hunting and incident response.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: The durability of CrowdStrike's moat is substantial, built on immense data network effects and the high switching costs associated with enterprise security infrastructure.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Once the Falcon lightweight agent is deployed across an enterprise's endpoints, ripping and replacing the entire security apparatus is extremely disruptive, costly, and risky.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: The Threat Graph architecture creates massive, compounding network effects. Every new customer endpoint analyzed improves the platform's overall threat detection algorithms, instantly benefiting all other customers globally.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Its proprietary AI algorithms and the massive dataset collected by its agents provide a strong intellectual property moat. Its cyberattack response expertise is widely recognized, even serving government agencies.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: As a pure-play cloud software business, CrowdStrike operates with high gross margins and tremendous capital efficiency, allowing rapid scaling with low physical infrastructure requirements.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Sentiment has largely recovered following the 2024 outage incident. The company's strategic partnerships and ongoing AI integrations act as robust catalysts.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Analysts maintain a highly positive outlook, expecting the company to sustain high free cash flow margins and continuous module adoption among existing customers.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: Recent news, such as the AWS and NVIDIA startup accelerator, reinforces CrowdStrike's narrative as an innovative industry leader at the forefront of AI-driven security.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Under CEO George Kurtz, the company has executed exceptionally well, successfully navigating past the 2024 outage crisis and continuing to push the technological boundaries of the Falcon platform.

prospectKeyRisks: Intense competition from well-capitalized tech giants, particularly Microsoft, who bundle security solutions with enterprise software packages.
The reputational damage of future software deployment failures or unmitigated breaches, as seen in the July 2024 faulty update incident.
A broader contraction in enterprise IT and cybersecurity spending in a weak macroeconomic environment.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Increased module adoption among existing customers as they consolidate their cybersecurity toolsets onto the single Falcon platform.
Strategic partnerships with AWS, NVIDIA, and other hyper-scalers to embed security deeper into modern cloud and AI infrastructure.
The escalating sophistication of state-sponsored and criminal cyber threats driving a secular, non-discretionary need for advanced endpoint protection.
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored CRWD at 86/100 and Opus at 87/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
